content: I've been getting into leatherworking lately. Made my first wallet last week and it actually turned out pretty decent!
That's awesome! Leatherworking is such a satisfying craft. What kind of leather did you use for your wallet?
Vegetable-tanned cowhide from a local supplier in Austin. I'm thinking of making a belt next, but I need to practice my stitching more.
Vegetable-tanned is perfect for beginners. Have you tried saddle stitching yet? It's stronger than machine stitching for leather goods.

Yeah, I've been practicing saddle stitching. Takes forever but the results are worth it. Between leatherwork and training for the Mellow Johnny's Classic next month, I barely have time for anything else.
The Mellow Johnny's Classic? That's quite the race! How's your training going?
It's brutal. I'm doing the 60-mile route this year. Last weekend I did a 40-miler and my legs were jelly for days. But it beats sitting at my desk all day analyzing genomic data at BioGenesis.
Sounds like you have a perfect balance of mental and physical challenges! What got you into leatherworking in the first place?

Honestly? My fiancé Sarah mentioned she wanted a custom leather-bound wedding guest book. I thought, why buy it when I can make it? Now I'm hooked. Planning to make all the groomsmen gifts too - probably leather coasters or something.
That's such a thoughtful wedding project! Custom leather gifts are so meaningful. Are you doing the wedding next summer in Austin too?
